stage.name: Character-to-Godot MVP
stage.targetUser: Solo and small-team game developers without a dedicated pixel artist who accept the LPC visual language
stage.qualityGate: Every in-scope task flow and expected behavior passes through visible interaction in the production UI with no unresolved severity 0-2 anomaly

promises.statement: A developer can begin with a useful animated character instead of an empty art canvas.
promises.user: A developer without production-ready character art
promises.context: Starting a local game project or prototype
promises.trigger: They need a coherent playable character quickly
promises.outcome: A named local project contains a composed humanoid character using a curated pack and sensible defaults
promises.quality: The first useful preview requires no asset-format, frame-layout, or licensing knowledge
promises.recovery: The user can cancel creation, replace choices, or return to a valid starter composition
promises.evidence: TF-FIRST-PROJECT and TF-COMPOSE pass in the actual UI

promises.statement: A developer can make the character fit their game while remaining visually coherent.
promises.user: A developer adapting a starter character
promises.trigger: The default character does not match the intended role or palette
promises.outcome: The user selects compatible layers, applies semantic colors, overrides one character, and previews idle and walk in four directions
promises.quality: Every action updates a crisp pixel preview immediately and exposes coverage or compatibility problems before export
promises.recovery: Selections and overrides can be replaced, cleared, or reset without rebuilding the project
promises.evidence: TF-COMPOSE, TF-EDIT-COMPOSITION, TF-THEME, and TF-PREVIEW pass

promises.statement: A developer can leave and return without losing the recipe or receiving a different character.
promises.context: Continuing work in the same browser on the same device
promises.trigger: The page is reloaded or the project is reopened
promises.outcome: Project name, pack, layer recipe, theme, overrides, preview settings, and deterministic render are restored
promises.quality: The same project data and pack version produce the same rendered image and export hashes
promises.recovery: Invalid or obsolete saved data is rejected with a clear recovery path and never silently corrupts a valid project

promises.statement: A developer can take the character into a game without manually slicing frames or reverse-engineering animation metadata.
promises.user: A Godot 4 developer or a developer using generic sprite tooling
promises.context: The character is ready to integrate
promises.outcome: The product downloads either a generic package or a Godot 4 package containing a spritesheet, animation data, build manifest, and credits
promises.quality: Names, frame regions, direction cycles, speeds, dimensions, and hashes are explicit and machine-consumable
promises.recovery: Export is blocked with actionable readiness messages when required content is absent
promises.evidence: TF-EXPORT-GENERIC and TF-EXPORT-GODOT pass, and the Godot fixture consumes the package

promises.statement: A developer can understand and carry forward the provenance and attribution attached to the chosen art.
promises.user: A developer responsible for shipping legally usable assets
promises.context: Selecting source art and exporting a character
promises.trigger: The user inspects an asset, credits, or an export package
promises.outcome: Every selected layer traces to pack, source, author, license, and chosen license path; exact merged credits ship with every export
promises.quality: Unknown or disallowed provenance fails closed rather than producing incomplete attribution
promises.recovery: The user can replace an affected layer with an eligible option

scenarios.success: A named project opens with a rendered starter character
Compatible choices are reflected in the preview and recipe

scenarios.intent: Match a project palette and personalize one character
scenarios.success: Project tokens and character override visibly resolve
Idle and walk play in every direction at chosen speed and zoom
The complete recipe and identical preview restore
ZIP contains PNG, animation JSON, manifest, and credits

scenarios.intent: Use the character without manual slicing
scenarios.success: ZIP imports into the fixture with named animations and frame regions
Selected sources and merged obligations are visible and exported

scenarios.actor: Developer encountering incomplete or invalid state
scenarios.startingState: Missing required layer or unreadable saved record
scenarios.intent: Return to a safe usable state
scenarios.success: No invalid export occurs and an actionable recovery completes

scenarios.intent: Use another pack through the same workflow
scenarios.success: Pack changes, compatible defaults load, and provenance/export update without a different workflow

capabilities.statement: User can create a named local project from a valid starter recipe.
User can cancel project creation without creating data.
User can rename the open project.
User can select an installed content pack and receive compatible defaults.
User can select an asset for each supported content slot.
User can clear or replace a selected layer; clearing a required layer exposes an exact readiness blocker until restored.
User can see required-slot, compatibility, and animation-coverage status before export.
User can inspect the selected asset's pack, author, source, license, and animation coverage.
User can apply a complete project theme preset.
User can edit semantic project color tokens using accessible color controls.
User can override a project token for the current character.
User can reset a character override to the project value.
User can select idle or walk animation.
User can preview north, east, south, or west direction.
User can play, pause, change speed, and change pixel-perfect zoom without layout shift.
User can explicitly save the complete project recipe locally.
User can reload or reopen the saved project and receive the same render.
User can recover from incomplete or unreadable local state without silent data loss.
User can download a generic export package.
User can download a Godot 4 export package with no manual frame slicing.
User can see export readiness and resolve each blocking item.
User can inspect exact merged credits for the current recipe before export.
User receives machine-readable and human-readable credits with every export.
User can complete the same composition, preview, persistence, and export workflow with a second pack.
User can complete the core create-to-export journey with keyboard-visible focus and no pointer-only control.

navigation.label: Project
navigation.backBehavior: Returns to the prior workflow view without discarding draft state

navigation.label: Preview
navigation.backBehavior: Playback pauses only when reduced motion requires it

navigation.label: Export
navigation.backBehavior: No package is generated until commanded

views.name: Project home
views.purpose: Create, name, save, reopen, and recover local work

views.name: Composition workspace
views.purpose: Choose packs and layers while inspecting compatibility and provenance

views.name: Theme workspace
views.purpose: Apply project tokens and character overrides

views.name: Animation preview
views.purpose: Evaluate supported motion at pixel-perfect zoom

views.name: Export and credits
views.purpose: Resolve readiness, inspect credits, and produce generic or Godot packages

expectedBehaviors.oracle: Activating New project opens a named dialog, focuses Project name, and leaves storage unchanged.
Cancel closes the dialog, returns focus to New project, and creates no project.
Project name accepts trimmed text; Create project remains disabled for whitespace-only input.
Create project opens Project home with the exact name and a visible nonblank starter preview.
Compose navigation becomes current and the persistent preview remains nonblank without horizontal overflow.
Changing pack replaces incompatible selections with declared defaults and announces the change.
Selecting each slot exposes only assets declared for that slot and identifies the selected asset.
Selecting an asset changes visible preview pixels, selected state, recipe state, and status synchronously.
Clear removes an optional layer, changes preview pixels, and does not affect required layers.
Choosing another option replaces rather than stacks the slot and updates the selected control.
Asset details visibly names pack, author, source, chosen license, and idle/walk coverage for the selection.
Composition status lists zero blockers for a complete recipe and exact missing required slots for an incomplete recipe.
Theme navigation becomes current and exposes all six semantic token groups.
Selecting a preset updates all token values and visibly changes the preview in one committed action.
Entering a valid six-digit hex updates the named project token; invalid input exposes an inline error and does not change pixels.
Enabling an override stores a character value distinct from the project value and labels its source as Character.
Reset removes the override, restores the project token visibly, and disables Reset until another override exists.
Preview navigation becomes current and canvas dimensions remain stable during control changes.
Idle exposes one stable pose; Walk advances through the declared four-frame cycle.
Each direction control produces the declared orientation and remains selected through pause/play.
Pause freezes the frame index; Play resumes from that index and accessible name reflects the next action.
Changing speed updates the visible multiplier and measured frame interval within 20 percent.
Zoom changes by integer scale, uses nearest-neighbor pixels, and causes no overlap or page overflow.
Save writes the complete versioned project, changes status to Saved, and disables until another edit.
Reload restores exact project name, selections, token values, overrides, preview state, and render hash.
Rename validates nonblank input, updates shell and saved record, and preserves character state.
Export view shows Ready only when every required slot and provenance record is valid.
Download produces one ZIP with spritesheet.png, animations.json, build-manifest.json, credits.json, and CREDITS.txt.
Godot export command is enabled exactly when generic readiness passes and identifies Godot 4.x.
Download adds character_sprite_frames.tres and README-GODOT.md whose regions and animation names match animations.json.
Credits preview lists each unique selected asset source and chosen license with no unselected source.
Replacing a layer removes its obsolete source and adds the new source before export.
Removing a required layer changes readiness to Blocked, lists the exact slot, and disables both downloads.
Restore safe project replaces invalid state with valid defaults only after explicit activation and announces recovery.
Selecting the second pack updates pack ID, all compatible defaults, preview pixels, and provenance through the same controls.
The second-pack recipe reaches Ready and downloads the same generic file contract without a pack-specific UI.
Keyboard-only interaction creates a project, changes a layer and theme, previews walk, and starts export with visible focus and no trap.

flows.completion: Edited recipe with accurate status and provenance
Resolved project theme and optional character override
Every required animation and direction inspected
Reloaded project has identical state and render
Visible credits exactly match selected sources
Invalid state is blocked then explicitly restored

steps.action: Remove a required layer and inspect Export

flows.completion: Second pack follows the same complete contract
Generic export command reached without pointer

exclusions.reason: Reasonable for the North Star studio but not required to prove the complete character-to-engine promise
exclusions.consequence: MVP projects contain characters only

exclusions.reason: Requires a distinct producer and perspective constraints unrelated to the first character journey
exclusions.consequence: No scene furnishing in MVP

exclusions.reason: One character does not need a scene graph to reach Godot
exclusions.consequence: Preview is character-only

exclusions.reason: The promise is selection and semantic recoloring, not replacing a dedicated pixel editor
exclusions.consequence: Off-catalog art must be edited externally

exclusions.reason: MVP consumes declared pack animations and only previews them
exclusions.consequence: Idle and walk timing can be selected only through pack data

exclusions.expectation: Generate normal, specular, or emissive maps
exclusions.reason: Unlit character delivery is complete without speculative material inference
exclusions.consequence: Exports contain diffuse sprites only

exclusions.reason: Requires different frames, masks, and compositing rules that do not prove the humanoid MVP
exclusions.consequence: Subject type is humanoid only

exclusions.reason: Different animation contracts should be learned from a later real implementation
exclusions.consequence: No alternate skeletons

exclusions.expectation: Preview spellcast, thrust, shoot, slash, hurt, run, climb, or jump
exclusions.reason: Idle and walk are sufficient to prove four-direction animation, export, and engine import
exclusions.consequence: Packs may carry only idle and walk in MVP

exclusions.reason: Oversize sheets introduce frame geometry not needed by the first two animations
exclusions.consequence: Equipment is limited to non-oversize tools or decorative layers

exclusions.expectation: Create, install, or validate user-authored packs
exclusions.reason: A second built-in pack proves the data boundary before exposing authoring UX
exclusions.consequence: Only bundled packs are selectable

exclusions.reason: Godot is the deliberately chosen engine proof and generic JSON remains portable
exclusions.consequence: Unity users consume the generic package manually

exclusions.expectation: Automatically update a running engine project
exclusions.reason: A deterministic downloadable Godot package closes the MVP integration promise without filesystem permissions or plugins
exclusions.consequence: Users re-export after edits

exclusions.expectation: Drive generation through a public CLI or API
exclusions.reason: The headless core is architecturally callable but the customer MVP is the interactive journey
exclusions.consequence: Automation is internal to tests only

exclusions.reason: Bulk policies and uniqueness controls are a separate user job
exclusions.consequence: Characters are composed one at a time

exclusions.expectation: Sync projects through an account or cloud service
exclusions.reason: Local persistence satisfies return use without identity, infrastructure, or privacy scope
exclusions.consequence: Projects remain in the current browser

exclusions.reason: The target user is solo/small-team and collaboration does not affect character correctness
exclusions.consequence: No multi-user state

exclusions.reason: Pack consumption must prove stable before distribution economics
exclusions.consequence: No remote catalog

exclusions.expectation: Manage several saved projects in an in-app library
exclusions.reason: One named local project proves create, rename, save, reload, recovery, and deterministic rebuild without adding library lifecycle behavior
exclusions.consequence: Saving a newly created project replaces the current local saved project

exclusions.expectation: Manage several character recipes inside one project
exclusions.reason: One complete character is sufficient to prove pack composition, project theming, persistence, export, and provenance end to end
exclusions.consequence: The MVP project contains one Hero recipe

workPackages.gate: Deterministic pack, recipe, render, palette, and provenance checks pass
Downloaded artifacts and Godot fixture pass
Second pack completes the same UI and export contract without core branches

environment.browser: Playwright Chromium desktop plus 390x844 constrained viewport
environment.productServer: Vite development server owned by Playwright

issues.disposition: Resolved: narrowed ambiguous text locators to their owning control without changing product behavior.
Resolved: removed mobile top-bar and workflow-tab intrinsic widths that forced a 426px layout at a 390px viewport.
Resolved: test setup now clears storage once rather than deleting saved data on every reload.
Resolved: persistence capture waits for the selected composition render hash to commit before saving.
Resolved: fixture mirrors root package extraction and defers resource loading until Godot import remaps are initialized.

decisions.decision: Use a local-first React and TypeScript Vite application with Canvas 2D, JSON contracts, Vitest, and Playwright.
decisions.evidence: Browser rendering, local persistence, ZIP export, and actual-UI automation are all supported without a service.

decisions.decision: Bundle two original CC0-compatible demonstration packs with complete project-authored provenance, while retaining the manifest contract needed for later reviewed LPC corpus intake.
decisions.evidence: Eliminates unresolved third-party legal approval while proving composition, tokens, packs, attribution, and export end to end.

decisions.decision: Target Godot 4 text SpriteFrames resources in the MVP package and validate their declarations against the generic animation manifest.
decisions.evidence: Text resources are diffable and require no editor plugin or manual slicing.
